_meta.title: EarthPrint Shopping Spend-Based Emission Factors
_meta.primarySource: DEFRA 2023 GHG Conversion Factors — Scope 3 Spend-Based Factors
_meta.sheet: Scope 3 spend-based factors by expenditure category
_meta.notes: Spend-based factors are less precise than activity-based factors but useful when specific product data is unavailable (e.g. bank transactions). For USD, EUR, INR conversions: use purchasing power parity (PPP) adjustment or apply as approximate. Second-hand multiplier represents remaining transport/transaction emissions only.

factors.label: Clothing — fast fashion (new)
factors.notes: Includes manufacturing, dyeing, transport. Fast fashion has high turnover → higher per-spend factor.

factors.label: Clothing — sustainable / ethical brands (new)
factors.dataSource: DEFRA 2023 — Clothing (adjusted for lower-intensity production methods)
factors.notes: Estimated 30% reduction vs. fast fashion for certified sustainable production

factors.label: Clothing — second-hand / vintage
factors.dataSource: Transport + platform emissions only; production emissions allocated to original buyer per ISO 14044

factors.label: Electronics — large (TV, laptop, washing machine)
factors.notes: Manufacturing is carbon-intensive; high per-£ factor because devices have embedded energy

factors.label: Food delivery / takeaway
factors.dataSource: Estimated: DEFRA food service factor + delivery vehicle emissions
factors.notes: Includes food production + packaging + delivery vehicle emissions

factors.label: Services (subscriptions, entertainment, professional)
factors.notes: Services have much lower physical goods emissions; mostly energy and commuting
